1.gl: to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)
1.kj: bewray (self), that are bidden, call (for, forth, self, upon), cry (unto), (be) famous, guest, invite, mention, (give) name, preach, (make) proclaim(-ation), pronounce, publish, re
1.dv: a primitive root (rather identical with H7122 (קָרָא) through the idea of accosting a person met);

1.kj: [phrase] aloud, bleating, crackling, cry ([phrase] out), fame, lightness, lowing, noise, [phrase] hold peace, (pro-) claim, proclamation, [phrase] sing, sound, [phrase] spark, thun
1.dv: or קֹל; from an unused root meaning to call aloud;

1.gl: to broaden out the ear (with the hand), i.e. (by implication) to listen
1.kj: give (perceive by the) ear, hear(-ken). See H239 (אָזַן).
1.dv: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from H241 (אֹזֶן);

1.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
1.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

1.gl: to hurry; figuratively, to be eager with excitement or enjoyment
David, the youngest son of Jesse
1.dv: rarely (fully); דָּוִיד; from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד); loving;

2.gl: the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)

2.kj: burden, collection, sign of fire, (great) flame, gift, lifting up, mess, oblation, reward.

2.kj: branch, [phrase] foot, hand((-ful), -dle, (-led)), hollow, middle, palm, paw, power, sole, spoon.

2.gl: a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering (usually bloodless and voluntary)
2.dv: from an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. bestow;

2.gl: intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn
